chapter_title.ar: باب الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ دُخُولِ الْخَلَاءِ وَ عِنْدَ الْخُرُوجِ وَ الِاسْتِنْجَاءِ وَ مَنْ نَسِيَهُ وَ التَّسْمِيَةِ عِنْدَ الدُّخُولِ وَ عِنْدَ الْوُضُوءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The words (to be spoken) during entering the toilet, and during the exit, and the cleansing (afterwards), and the one who forgets, and Naming (Bismillah) during the entering and during the Ablution (Wudhu)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/56)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/56)
text: الِاسْتِنْجَاءُ بِالْيَمِينِ مِنَ الْجَفَاءِ . وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّهُ إِذَا كَانَتْ بِالْيَسَارِ عِلَّةٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The cleansing (of private parts) with the right hand is from the repugnance’. And it is reported that it is a valid excuse if there was an illness with the left hand’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that washing one’s self with the right hand after using toilet is of injustice.” It is narrated in another Hadith that it is permissible if there something is wrong with the left hand.”
